metadata.purpose: RAG knowledge base for TARA pipeline — LiDAR Sensor ECU domain knowledge
metadata.notes: Enriched with IEC 60825-1 laser safety classification (Class 1 eye-safe, AE/AEL pulse model), AUTOSAR AP NM UdpNM state machine, ara::com Ethernet point cloud streaming, ENISA adversarial ML threat landscape for LiDAR, R155 T16.3 and T8.2 sensor threat references. LiDAR operates as standalone sensor ECU publishing 3D point clouds to ADAS/Fusion ECU over Automotive Ethernet.

automation_level.description: LiDAR ECU serves as primary 3D environment sensing subsystem for partial to high automation — provides high-resolution point cloud data for object detection, free-space mapping, and localization in L2 through L4 systems
automation_level.l2_l3_role: Obstacle detection, object classification support, lane boundary mapping
automation_level.l4_role: High-definition 3D mapping, precise localization, simultaneous object tracking

ecu_overview.primary_function: Emits pulsed laser beams across a 360-degree or sector field of view, measures time-of-flight of reflected pulses to compute distance, and generates a 3D point cloud of the vehicle surroundings at high frame rates. Publishes point cloud data to ADAS/Fusion ECU over Automotive Ethernet using ara::com SOME/IP. Must comply with IEC 60825-1 Class 1 laser safety classification — Accessible Emission (AE) must remain below Accessible Emission Limit (AEL) under all operating and fault conditions.
ecu_overview.core_pipeline: Laser Emission (pulsed) -> ToF Measurement -> Point Cloud Generation -> Filtering & Segmentation -> ara::com SOME/IP Publication -> ADAS Fusion ECU
ecu_overview.operating_environment: All-weather, day/night, highway and urban environments; operates continuously during vehicle motion; real-time hard deadlines for point cloud publication
key_characteristics: Pulsed laser emission — Class 1 eye-safe per IEC 60825-1 under all operating conditions
Accessible Emission (AE) continuously controlled to stay below AEL limits
Scanning or solid-state multi-beam architecture — treated as extended/distributed source per IEC 60825-1
3D point cloud output over Automotive Ethernet at 10–20 Hz frame rate
High bandwidth Ethernet interface — primary communication via ara::com SOME/IP
Network Management via AUTOSAR AP UdpNM — coordinated sleep/wake with ADAS ECU
6-DOF extrinsic calibration stored in ara::per (rotation + translation relative to vehicle frame)
Tight timestamp synchronization with cameras and radar via IEEE 802.1AS gPTP
POSIX-based OS (QNX or Linux RT) running AUTOSAR AP stack

hardware.mcu: Embedded SoC with DSP/FPGA accelerator for real-time ToF computation and point cloud processing, plus HSM for cryptographic operations

processing_units.role: AUTOSAR AP OS execution, ara:: service orchestration, NM state machine, application logic
Real-time Time-of-Flight (ToF) distance computation from raw pulse return signals — processes millions of returns per second
Controls pulsed laser emission — enforces AE within AEL limits per IEC 60825-1; governs pulse duration, repetition rate, and grouping
Cryptographic operations and key storage — supports ara::crypto for secure boot, TLS, and OTA verification

sensors.function: Emits pulsed laser beams — Class 1 eye-safe per IEC 60825-1. Pulse parameters (duration, repetition, grouping) are controlled to maintain AE below AEL under normal, maintenance, and fault conditions
sensors.iec60825_compliance: AE must not exceed AEL at any measurement aperture and distance under all operating and fault conditions including diffuser failure

sensors.function: Detects reflected laser pulses and measures return time — raw ToF data fed to DSP for distance computation
Steers laser beam across field of view — rotating (mechanical) or solid-state (MEMS/OPA). IEC 60825-1 extended source analysis applies to scanning multi-beam arrays

actuator_interfaces: Ethernet to ADAS/Fusion ECU for point cloud data publication
communication_interfaces: Ethernet (primary — 1GbE or 10GbE for high-bandwidth point cloud streaming via SOME/IP)
Ethernet (AUTOSAR AP UdpNM — network management coordination with ADAS and Fusion ECUs)
CAN (optional secondary — low-bandwidth status, health, and diagnostic triggers from BCM/GW)
IEEE 802.1AS gPTP (time synchronization for point cloud timestamp alignment with cameras and radar)

memory.flash: NVM for LiDAR firmware, DSP algorithms, and OTA updates
memory.ram: High-speed RAM for point cloud frame buffers and real-time ToF computation
memory.secure_storage: HSM-protected key vault for secure boot credentials and TLS keys
memory.persistent_storage: ara::per managed storage for 6-DOF extrinsic calibration matrices and operational configuration

iec60825_safety_mechanisms.protective_housing: Sealed housing prevents access to beam path during operation — IEC 60825-1 protective enclosure requirement
iec60825_safety_mechanisms.beam_stop_attenuator: Hardware beam attenuator that activates on fault conditions — prevents AE exceeding AEL on housing breach
iec60825_safety_mechanisms.emission_interlock: Hardware interlock disables laser emission when protective housing is opened
iec60825_safety_mechanisms.power_limiting_circuit: Dedicated circuit enforces maximum laser power output — prevents excessive emission under any fault
iec60825_safety_mechanisms.fault_condition_compliance: System must remain Class 1 compliant even on diffuser failure or mechanical fault per IEC 60825-1 evaluation requirements

software.os: POSIX-compliant OS (e.g., QNX, Linux with RT patch)

ara_functional_clusters.role: High-bandwidth point cloud publication and service-oriented communication with ADAS/Fusion ECU
features: SOME/IP service communication for 3D point cloud event publishing
Service Discovery for dynamic ADAS/Fusion ECU subscription
Event-based point cloud frame publication at 10-20 Hz
E2E protection on point cloud data for safety-critical integrity
MACsec support for Ethernet point cloud link security
lidar_use: Publish 3D point cloud frames to ADAS Fusion ECU at every scan cycle
Publish LiDAR health status and fault notifications
Subscribe to calibration update commands from diagnostic/OTA services

ara_functional_clusters.role: Coordinated network sleep and wake management for LiDAR Ethernet network cluster
features: Decentralized UdpNM algorithm — each node operates independently
Three operational modes: Network Mode (FULL_COM), Prepare Bus-Sleep, Bus-Sleep
Network Mode substates: Repeat Message State, Normal Operation State, Ready Sleep State
NM message structure: Source Node ID + Control Bit Vector (CBV) + User Data
Periodic multicast NM messages keep network active — absence triggers sleep transition
NM timers: NM-Timeout Timer, Repeat Message Timer, Wait Bus-Sleep Timer
Partial Network Cluster (PNC) support — activates only required network segments
External wake-up via NM message reception triggers FULL_COM transition
Security events and violation messages reported per NM spec section 7.10
lidar_use: LiDAR ECU participates in Ethernet NM cluster with ADAS ECU and Fusion ECU
LiDAR requests FULL_COM when sensor is active and publishing point clouds
LiDAR transitions to Bus-Sleep when vehicle is parked and sensing is not required
NM state machine ensures LiDAR Ethernet link is coordinated with downstream consumers
PNC support allows LiDAR to sleep while other Ethernet services remain active
ara_functional_clusters.nm_security_note: NM messages over UDP/Ethernet are unauthenticated in base UdpNM spec — spoofed NM messages can force LiDAR into Bus-Sleep (DoS) or prevent sleep coordination (battery drain). Requires additional MACsec or application-layer authentication.

ara_functional_clusters.role: Manages LiDAR application and pipeline lifecycle
features: Process start/stop/restart
Execution dependency management
Function group state control for active/standby sensing modes
lidar_use: Start point cloud processing pipeline when vehicle ignition is on
Gracefully restart processing pipeline on DSP fault without laser emission interruption
Transition to low-power standby mode when LiDAR sensing not required

ara_functional_clusters.role: Health monitoring, fault reporting, and calibration access control
features: UDS and DoIP support
DTC for LiDAR-specific faults (misalignment, contamination, emitter failure)
Authentication and access control for calibration write sessions
Diagnostic conversations for extrinsic calibration read/write
lidar_use: Report LiDAR misalignment DTC when point cloud consistency check fails
Report laser emitter fault DTC when AE monitoring detects emission anomaly
Provide authenticated diagnostic session for 6-DOF calibration write after vehicle service
Report contamination/blockage fault (lens dirt, snow, ice)

ara_functional_clusters.role: Store LiDAR calibration and operational configuration
features: Key-value storage for 6-DOF extrinsic calibration parameters
File storage for point cloud filtering configuration
Encryption and redundancy for safety-critical calibration data
Versioning and update handling
lidar_use: Persist 6-DOF extrinsic calibration (rotation matrix + translation vector relative to vehicle frame)
Store point cloud filtering thresholds and segmentation parameters
Manage laser emission configuration and safety limits

features: TLS 1.3 for secure Ethernet point cloud communication
Digital signature verification for OTA firmware updates
Key management via HSM
HMAC for calibration data integrity
lidar_use: Secure Ethernet communication to ADAS/Fusion ECU
Verify OTA firmware package signatures before installation
Authenticate diagnostic session for calibration write access
HMAC protection of stored extrinsic calibration matrices

ara_functional_clusters.role: Runtime anomaly and security event monitoring
features: Ethernet traffic monitoring on LiDAR network link
NM message anomaly detection
Unauthorized service subscription detection
Security event reporting to vehicle SIEM
lidar_use: Detect unexpected point cloud subscription from unauthorized ECUs
Monitor for NM message spoofing attempts on LiDAR Ethernet cluster
Report anomalous diagnostic session attempts targeting calibration store

ara_functional_clusters.role: OTA update handling for LiDAR firmware and calibration
features: Secure software update pipeline
Rollback support for failed updates
Calibration data update verification
lidar_use: OTA updates for DSP point cloud processing algorithms
Remote calibration updates after vehicle service or alignment change
Firmware updates for laser driver and safety control circuits via TCU

ara_functional_clusters.role: Base C++ APIs for all LiDAR processing applications
features: Error handling (Result, ErrorCode)
Thread safety for parallel point cloud pipeline
Async handling (Future/Promise)
Logging and tracing
lidar_use: Safe async handling of high-rate point cloud frame buffers
Error propagation from DSP to diagnostic module on processing fault

ara_functional_clusters.role: Synchronized timestamps for multi-sensor fusion accuracy
svs_use: Synchronize LiDAR point cloud frame timestamps with camera and radar via IEEE 802.1AS gPTP
Ensure ToF measurement timestamps are aligned to vehicle time domain for accurate sensor fusion
Prevent fusion artifacts caused by timestamp skew between LiDAR and camera frames

ara_functional_clusters.role: Controls access to LiDAR services and calibration resources
lidar_use: Restrict 6-DOF calibration write access to OEM-authorized diagnostic sessions only
Control which ECUs may subscribe to raw point cloud stream
Enforce service access policies for laser emission configuration interfaces

application_layer.function: Controls pulsed laser emission — enforces IEC 60825-1 Class 1 compliance by monitoring AE against AEL. Manages pulse duration, repetition rate, and grouping. Activates beam attenuator on AE violation or housing fault.

Processes raw pulse return signals from photodetector array using DSP/FPGA — computes per-return distance from time-of-flight measurements, applies multi-pulse grouping logic per IEC 60825-1 pulse analysis model
Filters raw point cloud — removes outliers, rain/fog returns, ground plane points. Applies statistical and radius filtering to produce clean environment point cloud.
Segments filtered point cloud into clusters corresponding to distinct objects in the environment — provides preliminary object candidates for downstream ADAS fusion
Applies 6-DOF extrinsic calibration transformation (rotation + translation) to align point cloud to vehicle coordinate frame. Loads calibration from ara::per on startup.
Publishes calibrated 3D point cloud to ADAS/Fusion ECU via ara::com SOME/IP event at configured frame rate
Monitors laser emitter health, photodetector signal quality, scanning mechanism status, lens contamination, and AE compliance. Generates DTCs and health status reports.

Implements AUTOSAR AP UdpNM state machine for LiDAR Ethernet cluster — manages transitions between FULL_COM, Prepare Bus-Sleep, and Bus-Sleep modes based on sensing requirements and NM message exchange

assets.description: 3D point cloud generated from pulsed laser ToF measurements — primary output of LiDAR ECU published to ADAS/Fusion ECU over Ethernet
assets.cybersecurity_property: Integrity and availability are safety-critical — injected, removed, or manipulated points directly corrupt the 3D environment model used for object detection and path planning in ADAS

assets.description: 6-DOF extrinsic calibration parameters (3x3 rotation matrix + 3D translation vector) defining LiDAR pose relative to vehicle coordinate frame — stored in ara::per
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered 6-DOF calibration silently rotates or translates the entire point cloud in 3D space — all detected objects are systematically displaced in the vehicle frame without visible corruption. Attacker can cause ADAS to miss obstacles or react to phantom objects in wrong positions.

assets.description: Laser driver configuration — pulse parameters (duration, repetition rate, power level, grouping) that govern IEC 60825-1 AE/AEL compliance
assets.cybersecurity_property: Unauthorized modification of laser emission parameters can cause AE to exceed AEL — violates IEC 60825-1 Class 1 safety classification and creates eye hazard. Can also degrade detection range or saturate photodetector.

assets.description: LiDAR ECU software including AUTOSAR AP stack, DSP ToF algorithms, point cloud processing pipeline, and laser safety control logic
assets.cybersecurity_property: Unauthorized firmware modification can disable laser safety interlocks, corrupt point cloud output, introduce backdoors, or bypass IEC 60825-1 emission controls

assets.description: AUTOSAR AP UdpNM network state — controls whether LiDAR Ethernet cluster is in FULL_COM (active) or Bus-Sleep (inactive)
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed NM messages can force LiDAR into Bus-Sleep mode (denial of sensing), prevent coordinated shutdown (battery drain), or trigger unwanted wake-up cycles. NM messages are unauthenticated in base UdpNM spec.

assets.description: LiDAR SoC including DSP/FPGA, HSM, laser driver circuit, and IEC 60825-1 safety mechanisms (beam attenuator, emission interlock)
assets.cybersecurity_property: Physical compromise enables debug port attacks, safety circuit bypass (disabling laser interlocks), calibration extraction, and firmware replacement. Bypassing laser safety circuits creates IEC 60825-1 Class 1 compliance violation.

assets.description: Raw pulse return signals from photodetector array before DSP processing
assets.cybersecurity_property: Injected false pulse returns at the photodetector input level create phantom 3D objects or remove real obstacles at the earliest stage of the pipeline — hardest to detect as manipulation

assets.description: IEEE 802.1AS gPTP time synchronization used to timestamp LiDAR point cloud frames for multi-sensor fusion
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed gPTP grandmaster introduces timestamp offset on point cloud frames — desynchronizes LiDAR from cameras/radar in fusion pipeline causing misregistration of objects across sensors

data_flows.iec60825_note: AE must remain below AEL — controlled by Laser_Emission_Controller hardware circuit

data_flows.nm_note: UdpNM messages carry Source Node ID + CBV — unauthenticated in base spec, vulnerable to spoofing

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker uses an external pulsed laser source synchronized to LiDAR emission to inject false return pulses into the photodetector — creates phantom 3D objects or removes real objects from point cloud. Covered under ENISA AI Cybersecurity 2021 LiDAR spoofing.
attack_examples: Pulsed laser injected from roadside creates phantom vehicle in LiDAR point cloud
Spoofed returns remove pedestrian cluster from 3D environment model
attack_surfaces.iec60825_relevance: Attacker laser source itself may violate IEC 60825-1 — irrelevant to victim ECU compliance but relevant to physical attack characterization

attack_surfaces.description: High-power laser or strong light source saturates photodetector array causing total or partial blindness — point cloud returns null or degraded in affected sector. IEC 60825-1 does not protect against external illumination of the receiver.
attack_examples: High-power IR laser saturates photodetector — entire forward sector returns no valid points
Bright spotlight causes partial sector blindness — obstacle in that sector not detected

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker on vehicle Ethernet network intercepts, modifies, replays, or injects point cloud data between LiDAR ECU and ADAS/Fusion ECU
attack_examples: MITM replaces live point cloud with pre-recorded empty-road scan during approach to obstacle
Ethernet flood causes ADAS fusion ECU to miss LiDAR frame deadlines

attack_surfaces.description: AUTOSAR AP UdpNM messages are unauthenticated in base spec — attacker on Ethernet can send spoofed NM messages to force LiDAR ECU into Bus-Sleep, blocking point cloud publication
attack_examples: Spoofed NM message with CBV set forces LiDAR into Ready Sleep State — sensor goes offline
Continuous NM traffic prevents LiDAR from transitioning to sleep — battery drain attack
attack_surfaces.nm_spec_note: AUTOSAR AP NM spec section 7.10 includes security event reporting but base UdpNM provides no authentication — identified as architectural gap

attack_surfaces.description: UDS over DoIP allows read/write of 6-DOF extrinsic calibration — unauthorized write silently displaces entire point cloud in vehicle coordinate frame
attack_examples: Attacker in extended diagnostic session writes modified rotation matrix — all objects shifted 30cm in lateral direction
Calibration replay from previous session accepted without freshness check

attack_surfaces.description: Malicious OTA package replaces LiDAR firmware — can disable laser safety interlocks (IEC 60825-1 violation), corrupt point cloud pipeline, or introduce persistent backdoor
attack_examples: Backdoored firmware disables AE monitoring circuit — laser operates above AEL (Class 1 violation)
Modified DSP algorithm introduces systematic point removal for specific obstacle shapes

attack_surfaces.description: Physical access to LiDAR hardware allows bypassing IEC 60825-1 safety mechanisms — removing beam attenuator, disabling housing interlock, or overriding power limiting circuit to operate above Class 1 AEL
attack_examples: Beam attenuator physically removed — laser operates at full power above AEL
Housing interlock bypassed — laser active when casing opened for maintenance
attack_surfaces.iec60825_note: Physical bypass of safety mechanisms is a regulatory violation (IEC 60825-1) in addition to a cybersecurity threat

attack_surfaces.description: gPTP grandmaster spoofing introduces timestamp offset on LiDAR point cloud frames — desynchronizes LiDAR from cameras in sensor fusion causing 3D object position misregistration
attack_examples: 100ms gPTP offset causes LiDAR and camera to disagree on pedestrian position by 3 meters at 30 km/h
Intermittent gPTP disruption causes fusion pipeline to drop LiDAR data as stale

attack_surfaces.description: Debug ports on LiDAR SoC allow direct memory access, HSM key extraction, and firmware replacement — also enables direct manipulation of laser safety circuits
attack_examples: JTAG extracts HSM cryptographic keys — enables firmware signing bypass
Debug port used to patch laser emission controller to disable AE limit enforcement

threat_catalog.title: LiDAR Point Cloud Injection via Optical Pulse Spoofing
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses an external pulsed laser synchronized to LiDAR emission frequency to inject false return pulses into the photodetector — creating phantom 3D objects (fake obstacles, fake vehicles, fake pedestrians) or removing real objects from the point cloud by overwhelming genuine returns

threat_catalog.title: LiDAR Photodetector Blinding via High-Power Laser
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses a high-power laser or intense light source directed at LiDAR receiver aperture to saturate the photodetector array — causing complete or partial blindness in the affected sector. IEC 60825-1 governs the victim LiDAR's emission safety but provides no protection against external illumination of its receiver.

threat_catalog.title: Unauthorized 6-DOF Calibration Write via Diagnostic Session
threat_catalog.description: Attacker exploits an authenticated or unauthenticated UDS diagnostic session over DoIP to write modified 6-DOF extrinsic calibration parameters — causing the entire point cloud to be silently rotated or translated in the vehicle coordinate frame without any observable corruption

threat_catalog.title: UdpNM Message Spoofing Forcing LiDAR into Bus-Sleep
threat_catalog.description: Attacker on vehicle Ethernet sends spoofed AUTOSAR AP UdpNM messages with CBV forcing the LiDAR NM state machine into Ready Sleep or Bus-Sleep state — taking the LiDAR sensor offline and cutting off point cloud publication to the ADAS fusion pipeline. UdpNM messages are unauthenticated in base AUTOSAR NM spec.
threat_catalog.autosar_nm_note: AUTOSAR AP NM spec R25-11 section 7.10 provides security event reporting infrastructure but no authentication for NM messages — architectural gap requiring MACsec or application-layer MAC

threat_catalog.title: Malicious OTA Firmware Disabling Laser Safety Interlocks
threat_catalog.description: Attacker delivers malicious OTA firmware package that disables IEC 60825-1 safety mechanisms in the Laser_Emission_Controller — removing AE monitoring, disabling beam attenuator activation, or overriding power limiting circuit. Causes LiDAR to operate above AEL in Class 1 — creating an unlabeled eye hazard.
threat_catalog.iec60825_note: IEC 60825-1 requires Class 1 compliance under fault conditions — firmware that disables safety mechanisms is a regulatory violation in addition to a cybersecurity threat

threat_catalog.title: Point Cloud MITM Replacement on Ethernet Link
threat_catalog.description: Attacker performs man-in-the-middle attack on Ethernet link between LiDAR ECU and ADAS/Fusion ECU — replacing live point cloud stream with pre-recorded scan of a clear environment, causing ADAS to operate on falsified 3D data during approach to an obstacle

threat_catalog.title: gPTP Grandmaster Spoofing Causing Fusion Desynchronization
threat_catalog.description: Attacker spoofs IEEE 802.1AS gPTP grandmaster clock on vehicle Ethernet, introducing timestamp offset on LiDAR point cloud frames — causing sensor fusion pipeline to misregister LiDAR object positions relative to camera detections by meters at highway speeds

threat_catalog.title: Adversarial Point Cloud Input for ML Segmentation Evasion
threat_catalog.description: Attacker crafts physical objects with specific surface properties (retroreflective patterns, geometry) that cause the LiDAR segmentation ML model to misclassify or fail to detect them — analogous to adversarial patches for cameras but exploiting ToF geometry and intensity

threat_catalog.title: Laser Emission Config Tampering to Violate IEC 60825-1 Class 1
threat_catalog.description: Attacker with diagnostic or physical access modifies laser emission configuration parameters (pulse power, duty cycle, repetition rate grouping) causing AE to exceed AEL — LiDAR operates above Class 1 limits creating undeclared eye hazard. Pulse grouping rules per IEC 60825-1 mean seemingly small parameter changes can cause AE exceedance.
threat_catalog.iec60825_note: IEC 60825-1 pulse grouping analysis — multiple pulses close in time treated as effective single pulse; AEL comparison must use worst-case grouping. Small increases in repetition rate can trigger AEL exceedance.

threat_catalog.title: JTAG Debug Port Exploitation for Firmware and Safety Bypass
threat_catalog.description: Attacker with physical access to LiDAR ECU board exploits active JTAG debug port to extract HSM keys, flash modified firmware bypassing secure boot, and directly manipulate laser safety control circuits — enabling persistent, undetectable compromise of both cyber and laser safety properties

security_controls.control: Secure Boot with IEC 60825-1 Safety Lock
security_controls.description: AUTOSAR AP secure boot verifies firmware signature at startup using HSM — prevents execution of unauthorized firmware that could disable laser safety interlocks. Boot sequence also verifies integrity of Laser_Emission_Controller configuration before enabling emission.

Dedicated hardware circuit enforces AE/AEL compliance independent of software — beam attenuator activates automatically if AE monitoring detects exceedance. Housing interlock disables emission on case opening. Cannot be bypassed by firmware modification alone.
security_controls.autosar_mechanism: Hardware Safety Circuit — independent of AUTOSAR stack

security_controls.description: UDS Security Access (0x27) or Authentication (0x29) service enforces cryptographic challenge-response before extended or programming session — prevents unauthorized 6-DOF calibration writes and laser emission config modification
6-DOF extrinsic calibration matrices stored in ara::per are HMAC-protected — any modification detected on next read before applying transformation to point cloud

security_controls.control: TLS 1.3 on Ethernet Point Cloud Link
security_controls.description: Point cloud stream from LiDAR to ADAS/Fusion ECU transported over TLS 1.3 encrypted Ethernet — prevents MITM replacement and replay of point cloud data
IEEE 802.1AE MACsec provides Layer 2 authentication and encryption on LiDAR Ethernet link — protects against NM message spoofing, point cloud injection, and MITM attacks at network level

ara::idsm monitors NM message traffic for anomalous patterns — unexpected NM messages from unknown source node IDs, CBV manipulation attempts, and abnormal sleep/wake transition rates are logged and reported. Addresses architectural gap in base UdpNM authentication.
security_controls.autosar_mechanism: ara::idsm + AUTOSAR NM spec section 7.10 security events

security_controls.description: ara::ucm verifies digital signature of all OTA update packages using OEM root of trust in HSM before installation — prevents malicious firmware deployment that could compromise laser safety circuits

IEEE 802.1AS-2020 secured time synchronization authenticates gPTP grandmaster source — prevents spoofed time source injection that would desynchronize LiDAR timestamps from camera/radar fusion pipeline

security_controls.control: JTAG and Debug Port Fusing in Production
security_controls.description: All JTAG and debug ports hardware-fused (permanently disabled) on production LiDAR ECU units — prevents physical debug exploitation for HSM key extraction, laser safety bypass, and firmware replacement

Runtime monitor checks statistical properties of incoming point cloud (return density, range distribution, sector coverage) — detects anomalous injection patterns (sudden phantom cluster appearance, complete sector loss) and flags for ADSM reporting

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: Vehicle-frame 3D point cloud at 10-20 Hz — primary input to sensor fusion and object detection pipeline
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: TLS 1.3, E2E protection, SOME/IP service authentication

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: OTA firmware and calibration update packages
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: TLS 1.3, OTA package digital signature verification via ara::ucm

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: Diagnostic messages routed from external tester via DoIP; NM cluster boundary management
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: DoIP session management, UDS authentication, NM cluster isolation

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: UdpNM messages (Source Node ID + CBV) for coordinated network sleep/wake
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: MACsec Layer 2 authentication, ara::idsm NM message monitoring

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: IEEE 802.1AS time synchronization messages for point cloud frame timestamping

item_definition.item_boundary: Includes pulsed laser emitter array, photodetector array, scanning mechanism, IEC 60825-1 safety circuits (beam attenuator, housing interlock, power limiter), DSP/FPGA ToF engine, point cloud processing pipeline, AUTOSAR AP NM state controller, calibration store, and Ethernet publication service
item_definition.operational_environment: All-weather vehicle operation at SAE L2-L4 automation levels; point cloud published continuously during vehicle motion; NM-managed sleep during parking/ignition-off

iso21434_tara_context.asset_identification_method: ISO21434 clause-9 — Assets identified by CIA triad mapped to safety-critical sensing functions and IEC 60825-1 laser safety compliance requirements
iso21434_tara_context.threat_identification_method: STRIDE per asset combined with ENISA automotive AI threat taxonomy, UNECE R155 Annex 5 threat list, and IEC 60825-1 fault condition analysis

damage_scenario_examples.description: Vehicle collides with pedestrian because attacker injected phantom obstacle earlier causing ADAS emergency brake, then removed real pedestrian from point cloud during approach
Silent 6-DOF calibration tampering causes all LiDAR-detected objects to be displaced 0.5m laterally — ADAS path planning uses wrong obstacle positions across entire vehicle fleet after malicious OTA
LiDAR taken offline by NM spoofing during highway driving — ADAS fusion falls back to camera-only mode without driver awareness, degrading obstacle detection in adverse weather
Malicious OTA disables IEC 60825-1 beam attenuator — LiDAR emits above Class 1 AEL creating undeclared eye hazard to pedestrians and road workers
damage_scenario_examples.severity: Critical — personal injury liability and regulatory violation

cybersecurity_goals: CG1: The LiDAR ECU shall publish an authentic, unmanipulated 3D point cloud at all times during active sensing
CG2: 6-DOF extrinsic calibration shall be protected from unauthorized modification throughout the vehicle lifetime
CG3: Laser emission shall remain within IEC 60825-1 Class 1 AEL limits under all operating, fault, and update conditions
CG4: LiDAR firmware shall only be updated via authenticated and integrity-verified OTA packages
CG5: LiDAR Ethernet network state shall only be controlled by authenticated NM participants
CG6: LiDAR point cloud timestamps shall remain synchronized with vehicle time domain for accurate sensor fusion

iec60825_tara_mapping.laser_class: Class_1 — AE does not exceed AEL under normal use, maintenance, and fault conditions
key_tara_implications: Laser emission config is a safety-critical asset — tampering causes AEL exceedance (Class 1 violation)
Fault condition compliance required — firmware attacks that disable beam attenuator or AE monitoring are both cyber and laser safety threats
Pulse grouping analysis (IEC 60825-1) means small parameter changes in repetition rate can cause disproportionate AE increase — precision attack vector
Physical safety mechanisms (beam attenuator, housing interlock) must be tamper-evident and hardware-enforced, not software-only

autosar_nm_tara_mapping.spec: AUTOSAR AP SWS Network Management R25-11 — UdpNM
key_tara_implications: UdpNM messages are unauthenticated in base spec — NM spoofing is a viable DoS vector for LiDAR
NM state machine has three modes: Network Mode (active), Prepare Bus-Sleep, Bus-Sleep — attacker forcing Bus-Sleep takes LiDAR offline
Section 7.10 security events provide detection infrastructure but no prevention — must be supplemented with MACsec or application MAC
Partial Network Cluster (PNC) support must be configured to prevent unauthorized activation/deactivation of LiDAR network segment
